title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 210 SPI PIC Timeout
description: Error 210 SPI PIC Timeout indicates loss of communication between internal control components, leading to pump stoppage or failure to start infusion.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop infusion immediately, remove pump from patient use, and transfer therapy to a functioning pump.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 210 is displayed and observe when it occurs and how the pump behaves.

steps.title: Perform Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn off pump, disconnect power, wait 30–60 seconds, and restart.

steps.title: Check Power and Battery
steps.instructions: Use a known good outlet and verify battery condition and charging status.

steps.title: Inspect External Connections
steps.instructions: Reseat modules and inspect connectors for contamination or damage.

steps.title: Test Minimal Configuration
steps.instructions: Power on without accessories to determine if issue is externally influenced.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 210 SPI PIC Timeout; unable to start infusion.
CCR.Cause: Internal communication failure suspected after power cycle and external checks did not resolve issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair/bench evaluation.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 212 Sensor Task Timeout
description: Error 212 Sensor Task Timeout indicates a failure in internal sensor processing, causing infusion interruption or unsafe operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop infusion, remove pump from use, and transfer therapy to another device.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 212 is displayed and observe when and how it occurs.

steps.title: Perform Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn off, remove power, wait 30–60 seconds, and restart the pump.

steps.title: Check Power Stability
steps.instructions: Test operation on AC and battery power to rule out voltage instability.

steps.title: Inspect External Components
steps.instructions: Check cassette area, connections, and housing for damage or contamination.

steps.title: Test with Known-Good Setup
steps.instructions: Load a known-good IV set and test pump operation off-patient.

steps.title: Remove from Service
steps.instructions: If error persists, remove from service and send for repair.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 212 Sensor Task Timeout and stopping infusion during use
CCR.Cause: Internal sensor task timing failure; error persisted after power cycle and external checks
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent to vendor/biomed repair for evaluation

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 213 Battery Task Timeout
description: Error 213 Battery Task Timeout indicating battery communication or power management failure causing alarms or pump operation issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from use and transfer infusion to a working device.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm error appears and note when it occurs and associated symptoms.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Plug into known-good outlet and verify charging indicator.

steps.title: Inspect Power Adapter
steps.instructions: Check for damage or loose connections and test with known-good adapter.

steps.title: Evaluate Battery Charging
steps.instructions: Observe charging behavior and test pump operation on battery.

steps.title: Reseat or Replace Battery
steps.instructions: Inspect contacts and install known-good battery if available.

steps.title: Perform Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Restart device after removing power for 30–60 seconds.

steps.title: Isolate Battery vs Internal Issue
steps.instructions: Determine if issue occurs only on battery or also on AC power.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 213 Battery Task Timeout with intermittent power concerns.
CCR.Cause: Battery failure or internal battery communication timeout observed during testing.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery and tested operation; if unresolved, removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 240 Key Buffer is Full
description: Error 240 Key Buffer is Full indicates excessive or continuous keypad input causing unresponsive controls, delayed entries, or unintended actions affecting pump operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient condition, transfer infusion to a backup pump if needed, and remove device from use if controls are unreliable.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm error display and observe keypad responsiveness, repeated inputs, and when the issue occurs.

steps.title: Check for Stuck Keys
steps.instructions: Inspect keypad for stuck buttons, debris, or damage and test key movement.

steps.title: Clean Keypad Surface
steps.instructions: Remove contamination using approved wipes and allow keypad to dry fully.

steps.title: Power Cycle Pump
steps.instructions: Turn off, disconnect power, wait 10–15 seconds, and restart to clear buffer faults.

steps.title: Remove External Interference
steps.instructions: Ensure nothing is pressing on the keypad such as lines, mounts, or covers.

steps.title: Test Keypad Functionality
steps.instructions: Verify normal operation through menu navigation and data entry.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 240 Key Buffer is Full with unresponsive keypad during programming.
CCR.Cause: Keypad input buffer overload likely caused by stuck key or internal keypad/interface failure.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned keypad, checked for obstructions, and power cycled unit. Error persisted, device removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 300 Fail to enable US sensor
description: Error 300 indicating ultrasonic sensor failure preventing fluid detection and safe infusion operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from use and transfer infusion to a working device.

steps.title: Verify Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 300 appears and note when it occurs and associated symptoms.

steps.title: Check IV Set
steps.instructions: Ensure correct, properly installed Baxter IV set is used and aligned in sensor area.

steps.title: Inspect Sensor Area
steps.instructions: Check for residue, moisture, or obstruction and clean if needed.

steps.title: Reseat and Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Reinstall IV set, close door securely, and power cycle the pump.

steps.title: Test with Known-Good Set
steps.instructions: Use a new IV set to determine if issue is accessory-related.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 300 ‘Fail to enable US sensor’; unable to initiate infusion.
CCR.Cause: Ultrasonic sensor failed to initialize despite IV set replacement, cleaning, and power cycle.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for repair. Backup pump provided.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 301 US upper avg high
description: Error 301 US upper avg high indicating abnormal ultrasonic sensor readings affecting fluid detection and infusion operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from patient use and transfer infusion to a backup device.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 301 is displayed and determine when it occurs.

steps.title: Check IV Set Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure correct tubing is installed and properly seated without kinks.

steps.title: Inspect for Contamination
steps.instructions: Check pump channel for fluid, residue, or moisture and clean if needed.

steps.title: Check for Air or Improper Priming
steps.instructions: Ensure tubing is fully primed and free of bubbles or foam.

steps.title: Swap IV Set
steps.instructions: Replace with a known-good set to rule out accessory issues.

steps.title: Power Cycle Pump
steps.instructions: Restart pump to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Evaluate Environment
steps.instructions: Check for vibration, interference, or temperature issues.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 301 US upper avg high and not allowing infusion start
CCR.Cause: External causes ruled out; persistent abnormal ultrasonic readings indicating likely sensor fault
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and sent for repair

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 302 US lower avg high
description: Error 302 US lower avg high indicates abnormal ultrasonic sensor readings that can prevent proper cassette detection and safe infusion operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from patient use and transfer infusion to a backup device.

steps.title: Verify Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 302 US lower avg high and observe when it occurs.

steps.title: Inspect Pump Exterior
steps.instructions: Check cassette area for debris, damage, or contamination.

steps.title: Check Set Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure correct, properly seated administration set is installed.

steps.title: Try Known-Good Set
steps.instructions: Replace with a new compatible set to rule out disposable issues.

steps.title: Check for Moisture
steps.instructions: Inspect for residue or fluid near sensor areas.

steps.title: Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Restart pump to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Compare Conditions
steps.instructions: Test behavior with and without sets installed.

steps.title: Assess Fault
steps.instructions: If persistent, suspect internal ultrasonic sensor failure.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displayed Error 302 US lower avg high and would not operate normally.
CCR.Cause: External checks completed; error persisted indicating likely internal sensor fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 303 Bad upper US rdg
description: Troubleshooting Error 303 indicating an invalid upper ultrasonic sensor reading affecting infusion and air detection.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from use and transfer infusion to a functioning device.

steps.title: Verify Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 303 appears and note when it occurs and associated symptoms.

steps.title: Check IV Set Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure correct set is installed and properly seated, especially at upper sensor.

steps.title: Inspect Tubing Condition
steps.instructions: Check for air bubbles, kinks, or contamination and replace if needed.

steps.title: Clean Sensor Area
steps.instructions: Inspect and clean upper ultrasonic sensor area, ensuring it is dry.

steps.title: Check Door Closure
steps.instructions: Ensure pump door is fully latched and applying correct pressure.

steps.title: Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Restart pump to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Swap IV Set
steps.instructions: Test with a new IV set to isolate accessory issues.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 303 Bad upper US rdg; infusion would not start.
CCR.Cause: Invalid ultrasonic sensor reading persisted after IV set replacement and cleaning, indicating internal sensor failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 304 Bad Lower US rdg
description: Error 304 Bad Lower US rdg indicating invalid lower ultrasonic sensor reading causing flow detection failure, false alarms, or pump stoppage.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from patient use and transfer infusion to a working device.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 304 is displayed and observe when it occurs and associated symptoms.

steps.title: Check IV Set Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure correct Baxter IV set is installed and properly aligned in the pump channel.

steps.title: Inspect Tubing Condition
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, air bubbles, or defects and replace if needed.

steps.title: Check Priming
steps.instructions: Ensure tubing is fully primed with no air gaps.

steps.title: Inspect Sensor Area
steps.instructions: Check for contamination and clean sensor area if needed.

steps.title: Power Cycle Pump
steps.instructions: Turn off, wait, and restart pump to clear transient faults.

steps.title: Test with Known-Good Setup
steps.instructions: Use a new IV set and run a test infusion off patient.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 304 Bad Lower US rdg and would not start infusion.
CCR.Cause: Lower ultrasonic sensor failure persisted after ruling out IV set, tubing, air, and contamination issues.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced IV set, verified setup, cleaned sensor area, and power cycled with no resolution. Removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 305 DS rdng out of range (low)
description: Error 305 indicates a low downstream sensor reading that can prevent infusion due to cassette, setup, or sensor-related issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the pump from patient use and transfer infusion to a backup device if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 305 is displayed and determine when it occurs and associated symptoms.

steps.title: Check IV Set Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure cassette is properly seated, aligned, and door is fully latched.

steps.title: Inspect IV Set Condition
steps.instructions: Check for damage, air, or improper priming and replace with a known-good set if needed.

steps.title: Check for Flow Issues
steps.instructions: Inspect tubing for kinks, clamps, or open-to-air conditions.

steps.title: Clean Sensor Area
steps.instructions: Inspect and clean the cassette channel if contamination is present.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Pump
steps.instructions: Restart the pump to clear any transient sensor errors.

steps.title: Isolate Cause
steps.instructions: Determine if issue is accessory-related or device-related based on testing with multiple sets.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 305 DS rdng out of range (low); infusion would not start.
CCR.Cause: Error persisted after multiple IV set replacements and proper loading; suspected downstream sensor fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 306 DS rdng out of range (high)
description: Error 306 indicates high downstream pressure or abnormal sensor readings preventing infusion due to occlusion or sensing issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop infusion, remove from patient use, and transfer therapy to a backup device.

steps.title: Verify Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 306 is displayed and observe when it occurs and associated symptoms.

steps.title: Check IV Set
steps.instructions: Ensure correct Baxter IV set is installed and properly seated.

steps.title: Inspect for Occlusion
steps.instructions: Check tubing, clamps, filters, and patient access for restriction.

steps.title: Inspect Pump Channel
steps.instructions: Check for fluid, residue, or debris and clean if necessary.

steps.title: Replace IV Set
steps.instructions: Install a new known-good IV set to rule out disposable issues.

steps.title: Power Cycle Pump
steps.instructions: Restart the pump to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Test Without Patient
steps.instructions: Run pump into a test container to isolate device vs patient-related issues.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 306 DS rdng out of range (high); infusion would not start.
CCR.Cause: High downstream pressure detected; external causes ruled out indicating possible sensor fault.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced IV set and checked tubing; removed pump from service when error persisted.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 309 No-tube rdg too high
description: Error 309 indicates the pump falsely detects tubing when none is present, preventing operation and compromising air-in-line safety detection.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the pump from patient use and transfer infusion to a backup device if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 309 is displayed and occurs consistently without tubing installed.

steps.title: Inspect the Pump Channel
steps.instructions: Check for fluid, debris, or residue in the tubing channel and clean as needed.

steps.title: Check for Moisture Intrusion
steps.instructions: Inspect for liquid or dampness in the sensor area and allow the device to dry.

steps.title: Verify Door and Latch Function
steps.instructions: Ensure the door closes fully and aligns properly with no gaps.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn the pump off, wait 10–15 seconds, and restart to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Test Without Tubing Installed
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump does not falsely detect tubing when none is present.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 309 'No-tube rdg too high' and indicating tubing present with no set installed.
CCR.Cause: False high reading from no-tube sensor after confirming clean channel, dry conditions, and proper door function.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for repair after confirming persistent sensor misread.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 314 DS no-tube elevated
description: Error 314 indicates the downstream pressure sensor reads high without tubing, preventing safe pump operation and self-check completion.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from patient use and transfer infusion to a backup device.

steps.title: Verify Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 314 appears during startup or with no tubing installed.

steps.title: Inspect Channel
steps.instructions: Check for contamination, debris, or residue and clean as needed.

steps.title: Check Mechanics
steps.instructions: Ensure no obstruction or misalignment in pump mechanism.

steps.title: Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Restart pump after removing power to reset sensors.

steps.title: Test Without Tubing
steps.instructions: Confirm error does not occur with empty channel.

steps.title: Test with Known-Good Set
steps.instructions: Load new tubing and verify normal operation.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 314 DS no-tube elevated during startup; unable to initiate infusion.
CCR.Cause: Downstream pressure sensor reading high with no tubing present; no external causes identified.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned channel and tested with no tubing and known-good tubing; error persisted. Removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 320 Latch Switch Error
description: Error 320 Latch Switch Error indicating disagreement between door latch switches, preventing safe operation or infusion.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from patient use and transfer infusion to a backup device.

steps.title: Verify Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 320 and observe when it occurs during door movement or startup.

steps.title: Inspect Door and Latch
steps.instructions: Check latch hook, alignment, and ensure proper closure.

steps.title: Check for Debris
steps.instructions: Inspect and clean latch and switch contact areas.

steps.title: Test Door Closure
steps.instructions: Cycle door open/close multiple times and check for consistency.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect hinges, housing, and latch components for wear or damage.

steps.title: Power Cycle Pump
steps.instructions: Restart pump to determine if issue is transient.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 320 Latch Switch Error and would not operate when door closed.
CCR.Cause: Latch switches not agreeing due to misalignment, contamination, or mechanical wear.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned latch area and verified alignment, or removed from service for repair if issue persisted.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 321 Link Switch error (up)
description: Error 321 indicating upper link switch not activating, preventing proper latch detection and infusion operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from patient use and transfer infusion to another device.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 321 is displayed and observe behavior during door movement.

steps.title: Inspect Latch Mechanism
steps.instructions: Check for debris, damage, or improper latch movement.

steps.title: Check Cassette Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure proper seating and full door closure.

steps.title: Clean Latch Area
steps.instructions: Remove debris or residue affecting switch activation.

steps.title: Check Alignment
steps.instructions: Verify proper door and latch alignment.

steps.title: Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Restart the pump to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Isolate Cause
steps.instructions: Determine if issue is mechanical, accessory-related, or internal.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 321 Link Switch error (up); unable to start infusion.
CCR.Cause: Upper link switch not activating; no external obstruction identified.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned latch, verified cassette placement, power cycled. Error persisted and device sent for repair.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 322 Link Switch Error (low)
description: Error 322 indicates the lower link switch is not activating, preventing proper cassette detection and safe pump operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from patient use and transfer infusion to a backup device.

steps.title: Verify Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 322 Link Switch Error (low) appears and determine when it occurs.

steps.title: Inspect Door and Latch
steps.instructions: Check for proper door movement, latch engagement, and mechanical integrity.

steps.title: Check for Debris
steps.instructions: Inspect and clean cassette area and linkage points if contamination is present.

steps.title: Verify Cassette Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure cassette is properly seated and door is fully latched.

steps.title: Test Alternate Cassette
steps.instructions: Use a known-good cassette to rule out accessory issues.

steps.title: Observe Linkage Movement
steps.instructions: Confirm internal linkage moves properly during door closure.

steps.title: Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Restart pump to rule out temporary faults.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 322 Link Switch Error (low) when loading cassette; unable to start infusion.
CCR.Cause: Lower link switch not activating after external checks, indicating likely internal switch or linkage failure.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned and inspected cassette area, tested with known-good cassette, and power cycled. Error persisted; removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 345 Thermistor Disparity
description: Error 345 Thermistor Disparity indicates mismatched internal temperature sensors, leading to pump shutdown or unsafe operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the pump from patient use and transfer infusion to another device.

steps.title: Verify Error
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 345 Thermistor Disparity is displayed and determine when it occurs.

steps.title: Power Cycle Device
steps.instructions: Turn off, wait 10–15 seconds, and power back on.

steps.title: Check Environment
steps.instructions: Ensure the pump is not exposed to extreme temperatures or heat sources.

steps.title: Inspect Pump Exterior
steps.instructions: Look for damage, cracks, or fluid ingress affecting internal sensors.

steps.title: Stabilize Temperature
steps.instructions: Allow the pump to sit at room temperature for 15–30 minutes.

steps.title: Functional Test
steps.instructions: Power on and verify if the error clears without patient connection.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 345 Thermistor Disparity at startup; unable to initiate infusion
CCR.Cause: Persistent thermistor mismatch after power cycle and environmental stabilization; likely internal sensor fault
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair

title: Baxter SIGMA Spectrum Infusion Pump - Error: 347 Check Battery
description: Error 347 Check Battery indicates a defective battery leading to power instability, inability to hold charge, or unexpected shutdown during operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove pump from patient use and transfer infusion to a working device.

steps.title: Verify Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error 347 appears and observe when it occurs and associated symptoms.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify outlet, adapter, and AC operation are functioning properly.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure battery is seated correctly and check for damage or corrosion.

steps.title: Check Charging Behavior
steps.instructions: Allow full charge cycle and observe indicator behavior and persistence of error.

steps.title: Test Battery Operation
steps.instructions: Run pump on battery only and observe performance.

steps.title: Swap Battery
steps.instructions: Install a known good battery to isolate fault.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displaying Error 347 Check Battery and shutting down when unplugged.
CCR.Cause: Battery failed to hold charge and error persisted until replaced.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery and verified normal charging and operation.
